channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: How rockets could travel to Mars in half the time with nuclear thermal propulsion
item.description: NASA plans to send crewed missions to Mars over the next decade – but the 140 million-mile (225 million-kilometer) journey to the red planet could take several months to years round trip. This relatively long transit time is a result of the use of traditional chemical rocket fuel.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: I love a good bargain and these 7 appliances are under AU$100 ahead of Prime Big Deal Days
item.description: Early October Prime Day discounts see handheld steamers, kettles and more get discounts of up to 51% off.
item.dc:creator: sharmishta.sarkar@futurenet.com (Sharmishta Sarkar), TechRadar

item.title: NYT Strands today — hints, answers and spangram for Sunday, October 6 (game #217)
item.description: Looking for NYT Strands answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, including the spangram.
item.dc:creator: marc.mclaren@futurenet.com (Marc McLaren), TechRadar

item.title: Is the stock market in an AI bubble?
item.description: When it comes to AI investments, “a lot of money is going to get wasted,” MIT professor Daron Acemoglu told Bloomberg.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Meta unveils Movie Gen, an AI model that can generate video with sound
item.description: Facebook owner Meta announced on Friday it had built a new AI model called Movie Gen that can create realistic-seeming video and audio clips in response to user prompts, claiming it can rival tools from leading media generation startups like OpenAI and ElevenLabs.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: AI-powered transcription leaks workplace secrets
item.description: Alex Bilzerian shared on X that the Otter AI platform shared confidential information, The Washington Post first reported.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: 10 tech tips and tricks everyone should know
item.description: There are a handful of ways you can make your tech experience less annoying, including through fixing autocorrect, turning off certain notifications and by creating folders on your iPhone.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Google ships Gemini 1.5 Flash-8B AI model
item.description: Google’s Gemini 1.5 Flash-8B AI model is now production-ready. The company said the stable release of Gemini 1.5 Flash-8B has the lowest cost per intelligence of any Gemini model. Availability was announced October 3.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Alphabet’s Waymo will expand its robotaxi fleet with Hyundai EVs
item.description: Alphabet’s Waymo will add electric vehicles from South Korea’s Hyundai Motor to its robotaxi fleet, the companies said on Friday, as Waymo expands in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Buttigieg dismisses Musk's Helene federal response claims
item.description: Department of Transportation Secretary Pete Buttigieg called out tech giant Elon Musk on Friday for making false claims about the federal government blocking flights with deliveries for Hurricane Helene victims.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Fiscal Service using death data to reduce improper payments
item.description: Tim Gribben, the commissioner of the Bureau of the Fiscal Service, said the bureau refined and reframed its progress statement for the first time since 2018. The post Fiscal Service using death data to reduce improper payments first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Civilization 7 makers work with Shawnee to bring sincere representation of the tribe to the game
item.description: MIAMI, Okla. (AP) — Shawnee Tribe Chief Ben Barnes grew up playing video games, including “probably hundreds of hours” colonizing a distant planet in the 1999 title Sid Meier's Alpha Centauri.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Social media suspensions may be linked to misinformation, not platform bias: Study
item.description: Social media platforms’ suspensions of accounts may not be rooted in political biases, but rather certain political groups’ tendency to share misinformation, according to a new study.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Musk to attend Trump rally at site of attempted assassination
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk said he plans to attend former President Trump’s rally in battleground Pennsylvania this weekend, when Trump returns to the site where he survived the first assassination attempt on his life.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Cyber Leaders Exchange 2024: NSA’s Kristina Walter on exposing ‘sophisticated’ cyber adversaries
item.description: The Cybersecurity Collaboration Center director pursues whole-of-government approach to helping agencies and industry tackle global cyberthreats. The post Cyber Leaders Exchange 2024: NSA’s Kristina Walter on exposing ‘sophisticated’ cyber adversaries first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Meet Spot, the LAPD's new crimefighting robot dog
item.description: The futuristic four-legged machine, which boasts the size and agility of a 70-pound golden retriever, has been met with both fascination and concern.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, The Hill

item.title: Austrian activist Schrems wins privacy case against Meta over personal data on sexual orientation
item.description: The European Union’s top court has said that social media company Meta can’t use public information about a user’s sexual orientation obtained outside its platforms for personalized advertising under the bloc’s strict data privacy rules.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: AWS Launches Amazon Bedrock for Enhanced AI Innovation in Korea
item.description: AWS introduces Amazon Bedrock to accelerate AI innovation in Korea, providing customers with high-performance foundation models and large language models.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Tener prisa por alcanzar el ROI de la IA puede salir caro
item.description: Muchas organizaciones han luchado por encontrar el retorno de la inversión (ROI) después de lanzar proyectos de inteligencia artificial (IA), pero existe el peligro de exigir demasiado, demasiado pronto, según la firma de investigación y asesoramiento de TI Forrester.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The EU wants to know more about social media algorithms
item.description: Via the Digital Services Regulation (DSA), the European Commission has requested information from Youtube, Snapchat and Tiktok about which parameters their algorithms use to recommend social media content to users.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Addressing the public sector’s penetration testing problems
item.description: While there’s no such thing as a silver bullet solution to secure the public sector, continuous pentesting addresses these issues head-on. The post Addressing the public sector’s penetration testing problems first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: How data brokers are fueling elder fraud in America
item.description: Criminals are increasingly turning to data brokers to provide access to private information that can be used to improve their scamming schemes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: OpenAI demands investors shun rivals such as Anthropic, Elon Musk’s xAI
item.description: OpenAI has raised $6.6 billion from investors like Thrive Capital and Tiger Global, but the AI company also sought assurances that investors would avoid funding five competing firms, according to a Reuters report.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Spider lovers scurry to Colorado town in search of mating tarantulas and community
item.description: It’s tarantula mating season on the Colorado plains, when male spiders scurry out of their burrows in search of a mate.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Peipert And Hannah Schoenbaum, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Hurricane Helene brings climate change to forefront of the presidential campaign
item.description: The devastation wrought by Hurricane Helene has brought climate change to the forefront of the presidential campaign.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Daly, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Biden signs bill cutting environmental reviews for semiconductor industry
item.description: President Biden on Wednesday signed legislation exempting semiconductor manufacturing facilities from environmental reviews — despite objections from some Democrats and green activists who have expressed concerns about the potential for toxic waste.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, The Hill

item.title: ChatGPT maker OpenAI raises $6.6 billion in fresh funding as it moves away from its nonprofit roots
item.description: OpenAI says it has raised $6.6 billion in venture capital investments as part of a broader shift by the ChatGPT maker away from its nonprofit roots.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Cyber Leaders Exchange 2024: CISA’s Jeff Greene on challenging the status quo
item.description: The new executive assistant director for cybersecurity at CISA details how sharing threat intelligence more broadly pays off. The post Cyber Leaders Exchange 2024: CISA’s Jeff Greene on challenging the status quo first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Beware of fraudsters posing as government officials trying to steal your cash
item.description: CISA warns of scammers impersonating its officials. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson urges people to be alert and verify contacts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tesla posts first quarterly increase in deliveries, but shares slump with investors hoping for more
item.description: Low interest financing, sweet lease deals, price cuts and free charging boosted Tesla’s global deliveries in the third quarter, the first increase this year for the electric vehicle maker.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DoD agencies confront zero trust challenges, misunderstandings ahead of 2026 deadline
item.description: The Defense Department’s zero trust framework is acting as a catalyst, driving mission owners to industry in search of zero trust solutions The post DoD agencies confront zero trust challenges, misunderstandings ahead of 2026 deadline first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Docker tutorial: Get started with Docker
item.description: Containers are a lightweight way to make application workloads portable, like a virtual machine but without the overhead and bulk typically associated with VMs. With containers, you can package apps and services and move them freely between physical, virtual, or cloud environments.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: It’s a wrap for the HoloLens 2 headset
item.description: Microsoft on Tuesday confirmed it is no longer producing the HoloLens 2, the company’s second-generation augmented reality headset first launched five years ago at Mobile World Congress (MWC) in Barcelona.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Australia's online dating industry adopts code of conduct to keep users safer
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — A code of conduct will be enforced on the online dating industry to better protect Australian users after research found that three-in-four people suffer some form of sexual violence through the platforms, Australia’s government said on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: For Fiscal Service, intelligent automation transforms federal workflows
item.description: At the Bureau of the Fiscal Service, automation is helping to reduce manual workloads, and to increase employee efficiency and morale. The post For Fiscal Service, intelligent automation transforms federal workflows first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Dana Sukontarak, Federal News Network

item.title: Appeals court reinstates Indiana lawsuit against TikTok alleging child safety, privacy concerns
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — The Indiana Court of Appeals has reinstated a lawsuit filed by the state accusing TikTok of deceiving its users about the video-sharing platform's level of inappropriate content for children and the security of its consumers’ personal information.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Two good Visual Studio Code alternatives
item.description: The conductor of my choir famously tells us singers, “I only want everything all the time.” Well, as a developer, my mantra for code editors and IDEs (integrated development environments) is exactly that: I only want everything all the time.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The Rise of Microclouds: A Shift in Cloud Computing Landscape
item.description: The emergence of microcloud providers is changing the public cloud computing landscape, offering alternative services and value-driven solutions for businesses.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Arkansas sues YouTube over claims that the site is fueling a mental health crisis
item.description: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — Arkansas sued YouTube and parent company Alphabet on Monday, saying the video-sharing platform is made deliberately addictive and fueling a mental health crisis among youth in the state.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, Associated Press, Fox 5
